inclusion_criteria: Population/setting: Participants are dental practitioners, dental office staff, dental patients, dental students, or dental educators in dental care settings (e.g., dental clinics, private practices, community dental services, dental schools).

Exposure/intervention: Examines sexual misconduct (e.g., harassment, assault, boundary violations) occurring in dental settings or evaluates an intervention/policy aimed at preventing, reducing, or responding to sexual misconduct; effectiveness studies include a comparator (e.g., control group or pre–post).

Outcomes: Reports at least one relevant outcome—prevalence/incidence of sexual misconduct, underreporting rates, emotional/psychological harm, professional disengagement (e.g., burnout, turnover, absenteeism), or intervention effectiveness.
Study design: Empirical primary research (quantitative, qualitative, or mixed methods), including observational studies, surveys, audits, trials, or pre–post evaluations.
exclusion_criteria: Wrong population/setting: Not conducted in dental care settings or does not involve dental professionals/staff/patients/students/educators.
Wrong focus: Does not assess sexual misconduct (e.g., focuses solely on non-sexual bullying/violence) and does not evaluate an intervention specifically targeting sexual misconduct.
Wrong outcomes or data: Does not report any specified outcomes or provides commentary/policy/legal discussion without empirical primary data.
Study type limitations: Secondary research (systematic/narrative reviews), editorials/opinions, single-case legal reports, or conference abstracts without sufficient extractable data.
screening_criteria.description: Screening to identify primary empirical studies in dental settings that assess sexual misconduct exposure or interventions and report prevalence, underreporting, emotional harm, professional disengagement, or intervention effectiveness, with appropriate comparators for effectiveness analyses.

included_articles.title: Inappropriate Patient Sexual Behavior in the Dental Practice Setting: Experiences of dental hygienists.
Sexual Harassment Issues Among Virginia Dental Hygienists.
Sexual harassment in dentistry: prevalence in dental school.
An International Survey of Female Dental Students' Perceptions About Gender Bias and Sexual Misconduct at Four Dental Schools.
Perceptions of Sexual Harassment in Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery Training and Practice.
